title: Melted Buttery Korean Parma Ham Bao
description: A solo dinner Korean dish featuring parma ham, salt baked to perfection, served in a lazy Susan spinner, this Jain friendly meal is perfect for a quiet night in. It is buttery, flaky, and full of flavor.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the bao dough to a thickness of about 1 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a slice of parma ham in the center of each dough circle.
Brush the edges of the dough with sesame oil.
Fold the dough over the parma ham to form a half moon shape, and press the edges together to seal.
Place the bao on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each bao.
Sprinkle the salt over the bao, making sure to get a little on each one.
Bake the bao for 15 minutes, or until golden brown.
While the bao are baking, mix the melted butter, gochujang, and sugar in a small bowl.
Remove the bao from the oven and brush the tops with the melted butter mixture.
Return the bao to the oven and bake for an additional 2 minutes, or until the tops are golden brown.
